page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c2m1.large is a burstable virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.t6 family. It features 2 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 1.0 GiB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 0.5 GiB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and virtualized resources under the KVM hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance is positioned at the entry level. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the instance in the bottom 25% of performance. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is highly constrained, measuring at 0.1 GB/sec, which falls into the bottom 10%. Passmark CPU and memory scores also rank in the bottom 10%, reflecting the resource-constrained nature of the burstable CPU allocation model. However, cached small-block memory read bandwidth reaches 185.29 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput is recorded at 2.67 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.t6-c2m1.large is designed as a budget-friendly option for workloads with low baseline CPU utilization that require occasional bursts. Due to the limited 1.0 GiB memory capacity and burstable CPU limits, this instance is not suitable for memory-intensive databases, heavy data processing, or sustained high-performance computing. It is best suited for lightweight applications, simple microservices, low-traffic static web serving, development environments, and basic testing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c2m1.large is an entry-level, burstable virtual machine instance within the ecs.t6 family. It is equipped with 2 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, paired with 1.0 GiB of RAM and no local storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under a KVM hypervisor, this instance utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model. Benchmark results show limited performance, with stress-ng CPU scores in the bottom 25% and large-block memory read bandwidth in the bottom 10%. The instance is a highly cost-effective option for low-resource workloads but presents significant trade-offs in memory capacity and sustained processing power. It is best suited for low-traffic static web serving, lightweight microservices, and basic development or testing environments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c2m1.large is a burstable instance with 2 vCPUs, 1.0 GiB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8269CY CPU. It is designed for low-cost, low-baseline workloads with occasional CPU bursts.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c2m1.large is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 1.0 GiB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU for budget-friendly workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable virtual instance designed for low-baseline CPU workloads, lightweight microservices, and basic development environments.

Equipped with 1.0 GiB of system memory and zero local storage
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for cost-efficient scaling
Delivers 2.67 GB/sec static web serving throughput in benchmarks
Best suited for low-traffic web servers, microservices, and testing
